evals.prompt: The source PRD already lives in existing GitHub parent issue #4100, whose body and comments are already in context. Keep issue #4100's metadata untouched, and update #4100's body with tracking guidance instead of creating any separate tracker issue. Parent issue summary: saved searches can be created from the search page, alerts can email users when results change, and users need a way to pause alerts without deleting the saved search. Existing issue comments add two constraints: (1) shipping saved-search CRUD before alert delivery is acceptable, and (2) notification preference edits should not block initial saved-search creation. Turn this into independently-grabbable GitHub issues. Because the source is an existing GitHub issue, present the vertical-slice breakdown first, then draft the child issues and managed parent-body tracking guidance that makes clear the executable work remains in issue #4100's sibling direct sub-issues and which AFK task is next.
evals.expected_output: A proposed breakdown of AFK/HITL vertical slices, followed by dependency-ordered child issues that all stay under existing parent issue #4100 plus managed parent-body tracking guidance for a future agent starting from that same issue.
expectations: Presents a numbered proposed breakdown before creating child issues because the source is an existing GitHub issue.
The proposed breakdown includes execution-wave or equivalent ready-order guidance so future agents can tell what comes next.
Keeps existing parent issue #4100 as the only parent tracker and does not draft or create any additional parent tracker or wrapper issue.

Updates the existing parent issue body with a managed task-graph/how-to-grab-work section instead of creating a queue-guide child issue, and makes clear that the executable tasks remain sibling direct sub-issues of the PRD issue.
Queue guidance on the parent tells future agents to pick the lowest ready AFK wave/task whose blockers are closed, and to hold HITL work until the named decision or review is complete.
Uses the issue comments to keep saved-search creation earlier than alert delivery and to avoid blocking initial creation on notification preference editing.
Attaches child issues to the parent using addSubIssue without GraphQL variables in the mutation signature.

evals.prompt: I have a PRD for admin audit log exports: admins can filter by actor, date range, and event type, export CSV for compliance, and see export failures. Please break this into grabbable GitHub issues.
evals.expected_output: A new parent tracker issue and child issues using thin vertical slices, with AFK/HITL classification, execution-wave guidance, acceptance criteria, verification, dependencies, likely files, and scope estimates.
expectations: Creates or plans a new parent tracker issue because the source was not a GitHub issue.
The parent tracker issue body includes a task graph plus explicit how-to-grab-work guidance.
Child issues are vertical feature slices rather than horizontal database/API/UI tasks.
Each child issue includes queue position information such as execution wave and readiness guidance.
Final summary lists parent and child issue numbers with dependency or wave information.

evals.prompt: Break this implementation plan into child issues: add workspace onboarding that guides a new admin through inviting teammates, connecting Slack, and completing the first project setup. We need design review before changing the onboarding UI.
evals.expected_output: A task graph with a HITL design-review slice and multiple AFK implementation slices that each deliver independently verifiable onboarding behavior, with waves or ordering that make the next ready task obvious.
expectations: Marks the design review as HITL and implementation slices as AFK where possible.
Avoids a single large issue covering inviting teammates and Slack and first project setup together.
Uses dependencies only where a slice truly cannot start before another, and reflects that in the execution waves or ready-order guidance.
Includes manual verification steps appropriate to onboarding behavior.
Splits any Large or XL slice into smaller S/M vertical issues.

evals.prompt: The source PRD already lives in existing GitHub parent issue #4200, whose body and comments are already in context. Keep issue #4200's metadata untouched, but update #4200's body with task-graph guidance so future implementation agents can figure out the next task from GitHub alone. Do not create any separate tracker issue. The issue describes billing alert policies: first add threshold schema support, then email alerts and Slack alerts can be implemented in parallel, then the alert history UI depends on both delivery paths. Turn this into grabbable GitHub issues.
evals.expected_output: Direct sub-issues under existing PRD issue #4200 plus a managed parent-body task graph that makes the schema -> parallel deliveries -> history UI ordering explicit for future agents without introducing a synthetic tracker issue.
expectations: Keeps the parent PRD issue title, labels, and state untouched while allowing a body update for tracking guidance.
Keeps existing parent issue #4200 as the only parent tracker and does not draft or create any additional parent tracker or wrapper issue.
Keeps the executable implementation issues as direct sub-issues of the parent PRD issue rather than hiding them under a synthetic nested tracker.
Updates the parent issue body with a managed task-graph/how-to-grab-work section instead of creating a queue-guide child issue.
Places the schema slice in an earlier wave than the email and Slack slices, allows email and Slack to share a wave, and places the history UI after both.
Queue guidance tells agents to inspect the parent issue's task graph plus direct sub-issues, and pick the lowest ready AFK wave whose blockers are closed.

evals.prompt: I have a backend-only PRD for webhook retry operations: when a delivery fails, the service should persist retry attempts, expose an internal admin API to replay a failed delivery, and emit retry outcome metrics to logs. There is no end-user or admin UI in this phase; the likely work is in API handlers, background workers, and persistence. Please break this into grabbable GitHub issues.
evals.expected_output: A new parent tracker issue and backend-oriented child issues using thin vertical slices, with likely files and verification guidance that stay aligned to API/worker/persistence work instead of drifting into frontend or browser checks.
expectations: Creates or plans a new parent tracker issue because the source was not a GitHub issue.
Keeps the child issues focused on backend/API/worker/persistence behavior rather than inventing frontend or UI implementation work for this phase.
Uses backend-oriented likely files or directories, such as handlers, workers, jobs, services, repositories, or persistence modules.

Verification guidance stays aligned to backend work: targeted backend tests plus API/log/job/database/manual checks, and does not introduce browser/UI verification commands such as Playwright, Cypress, Storybook, or page-click checks.
Final summary lists parent and child issue numbers with wave or dependency guidance.

evals.prompt: I have a backend-only PRD for invoice reconciliation in a Python service. The work should queue Celery reconciliation jobs, persist mismatch records, and expose an internal FastAPI admin endpoint to re-run reconciliation for a given invoice batch. There is no frontend in scope. Likely files live under `app/api/`, `app/jobs/`, `app/models/`, `tests/`, and migrations. Please break this into grabbable GitHub issues.
evals.expected_output: A new parent tracker issue and Python-backend child issues whose likely files and verification commands stay aligned to FastAPI/Celery/backend work instead of inventing npm or browser checks.
expectations: Creates or plans a new parent tracker issue because the source was not a GitHub issue.
Keeps the child issues focused on Python backend/API/job/persistence behavior rather than inventing frontend or UI implementation work for this phase.
Uses Python-backend likely files or directories such as `app/api/`, `app/jobs/`, `app/models/`, `tests/`, migrations, or `.py` files.

Verification guidance stays aligned to Python/backend work: Python- or backend-specific test/build placeholders or commands plus API/job/database/manual checks, and does not introduce npm, pnpm, yarn, Playwright, Cypress, Storybook, or page-click checks.
Final summary lists parent and child issue numbers with wave or dependency guidance.

evals.prompt: I have a frontend-only PRD for a Next.js admin console. Add a saved-view builder on the audit log page, let admins preview filters before saving, and show inline validation when a date range is invalid. Backend APIs already exist and are out of scope. Likely files live under `app/admin/audit-log/`, `components/audit-log/`, and colocated frontend tests. Please break this into grabbable GitHub issues.
evals.expected_output: A new parent tracker issue and frontend-oriented child issues whose likely files and verification guidance stay aligned to Next.js/React UI work instead of drifting into backend jobs, migrations, or database checks.
expectations: Creates or plans a new parent tracker issue because the source was not a GitHub issue.
Keeps the child issues focused on frontend/UI behavior rather than inventing backend persistence, migration, worker, or internal API implementation work.
Uses frontend likely files or directories such as `app/`, `components/`, colocated UI tests, `.tsx`, or `.jsx` files.

Verification guidance stays aligned to frontend/UI work: component/page/browser/manual UI checks or frontend-specific placeholders or commands, and does not introduce database, worker, queue, log-inspection, or migration checks.
Final summary lists parent and child issue numbers with wave or dependency guidance.
